name: Supabase
tagline: Postgres database, auth, storage, and edge functions.
security: Never expose SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY to the client — it bypasses RLS.
Enable Row Level Security on every user-owned table without exception.
Use the @supabase/ssr server client in Server Components and Route Handlers, never the anon client.
Treat NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY as public — security comes from RLS policies, not key secrecy.
Sign Storage URLs with short TTLs for private buckets; never return raw storage paths.

patterns.name: SSR auth with cookies
patterns.description: Use @supabase/ssr to maintain auth state across Server Components, Route Handlers, and Middleware.

patterns.name: Row Level Security
patterns.description: Author Postgres RLS policies so the database itself is the source of access truth.

patterns.name: Signed storage URLs
patterns.description: Generate signed URLs server-side for private file access; never expose raw paths.

patterns.name: Realtime channels
patterns.description: Subscribe to Postgres changes via Realtime channels for live data.
